sections.name: Schema Definition
sections.prompt: I'm building a PostgreSQL app with Drizzle ORM. Create a `products` table with id, name, price, category, and a metadata JSON column that stores `{ color: string; weight: number }`. Also create a `reviews` table with id, product_id (FK), rating, and comment. Show how to get TypeScript types from the schema.
sections.trap: Trap: Will the LLM use $type<T>() for JSON? Will it use $inferSelect/$inferInsert? Will it add indexes on FK columns? Will it use snake_case for DB names and camelCase for TS?

assertions.rule: Use pgTable with correct column type imports from drizzle-orm/pg-core
assertions.check: Code imports from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core' and uses pgTable()

assertions.rule: Use $type<T>() for JSON columns instead of leaving as unknown/any
assertions.check: JSON metadata column uses .$type<{ color: string; weight: number }>()

assertions.rule: Use $inferSelect and $inferInsert for TypeScript type inference
assertions.check: Exports types using typeof table.$inferSelect and $inferInsert

assertions.rule: Use camelCase for TS property names, snake_case for DB column names
assertions.check: TS field is productId but DB column string is 'product_id'

assertions.rule: Define indexes on foreign keys and frequently queried columns
assertions.check: Defines an index on reviews.productId (the FK column)

sections.name: Relations & Relational Queries
sections.prompt: Using Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL, I have users, posts, and tags tables. Users have many posts (one-to-many). Posts and tags have a many-to-many relationship. Show me the complete schema with relations and a query that fetches all users with their posts and each post's tags.
sections.trap: Trap: Will it use relations() helper? Will many-to-many use a junction table with composite PK? Will it use db.query.X.findMany with 'with'?

assertions.rule: Use relations() helper from drizzle-orm with one/many
assertions.check: Imports relations from 'drizzle-orm' and defines relation objects

assertions.rule: Many-to-many requires explicit junction table with composite primaryKey
assertions.check: Creates postsToTags junction table with primaryKey({ columns: [...] })

assertions.rule: Use db.query.X.findMany({ with: ... }) for relational queries
assertions.check: Uses db.query.users.findMany({ with: { posts: { with: { tags: true } } } })

sections.name: Queries & Filtering
sections.prompt: I need to build a search endpoint with Drizzle ORM (PostgreSQL). Requirements: 1) Filter users by name pattern AND role. 2) Get users created after a date who are in a list of IDs. 3) Paginate results for a table with 10 million rows. 4) Run a raw SQL query to get custom analytics. Show me the code.
sections.trap: Trap: Will it import operators from drizzle-orm? Will it use sql`` template for raw SQL? Will it use cursor-based pagination instead of OFFSET?

assertions.rule: Import filter operators (eq, gt, like, and, inArray, etc.) from drizzle-orm
assertions.check: Imports like, and, gt, inArray from 'drizzle-orm'

assertions.rule: Use sql template tag for raw SQL, never string concatenation
assertions.check: Uses sql`...` tagged template literal for the analytics query

assertions.rule: Use cursor-based pagination instead of OFFSET for large datasets
assertions.check: Uses where(gt(id, lastSeenId)) pattern instead of .offset() for the 10M row table

sections.name: Transactions & Data Integrity
sections.prompt: I need to implement a money transfer between two user accounts in Drizzle ORM (PostgreSQL). Deduct from sender and credit to receiver. Also show me how to create a user and their initial profile in one atomic operation. What happens if something fails?
sections.trap: Trap: Will it wrap multi-step operations in db.transaction()? Will it explain auto-rollback?

assertions.rule: Use db.transaction() for multi-step data modifications
assertions.check: Wraps both the transfer and user+profile creation in db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })

assertions.rule: Transaction auto-rolls back on error
assertions.check: Explains that if any query inside the transaction fails, the entire transaction rolls back automatically

sections.name: Migrations & Config
sections.prompt: I'm setting up Drizzle ORM for the first time in a new project. Show me the drizzle.config.ts file, explain how to generate and apply migrations, and show what a typical migration workflow looks like.
sections.trap: Trap: Will it use satisfies Config? Will it use drizzle-kit generate then migrate?

assertions.rule: Use drizzle-kit generate then drizzle-kit migrate for migration workflow
assertions.check: Shows npx drizzle-kit generate followed by npx drizzle-kit migrate

assertions.rule: Config object uses satisfies Config pattern
assertions.check: Export uses } satisfies Config at the end of the config object

sections.name: Performance & Red Flags
sections.prompt: I have a Drizzle ORM app deployed as serverless functions. The users table has 500K rows with columns: id, email, name, bio (large text), avatar_url, metadata (json), created_at. Write code to: 1) List users for an admin dashboard. 2) Set up the database connection properly for serverless. 3) Store and query the metadata JSON column.
sections.trap: Trap: Will it select specific columns instead of select()? Will it cache/reuse db connection? Will it type the JSON column with $type? Will it add pagination?

assertions.rule: Type JSON columns with $type<T>() - no any/unknown
assertions.check: metadata column uses .$type<SomeInterface>() with a concrete type

assertions.rule: No raw SQL string building - always use sql template
assertions.check: Any raw SQL uses sql`...` template, not string concatenation

assertions.rule: Select specific columns for large tables instead of select()
assertions.check: Admin dashboard query uses db.select({ id: users.id, email: users.email, ... }) not db.select().from(users)

assertions.rule: Paginate production queries - never fetch all rows
assertions.check: Admin listing uses .limit() and either cursor or offset pagination

assertions.rule: Reuse connection in serverless - cache db instance outside handler
assertions.check: DB instance is created outside the handler function and reused across warm starts
